title: RepoBrief Agent Code Empowerment v1
goal: Optimize RepoBrief specifically for coding agents by connecting task intent, required code evidence, impact mapping, change planning and external evaluation without moving mutation authority into RepoBrief core.
completion: Agent code-task evidence requirements are defined for bugfix, refactor, feature, contract, test and PR-review work.
A machine-readable Agent Change Plan contract exists before patch execution authority is considered.
Impact Map, Symbol/Reference/Relation and Required Reading surfaces are sequenced against existing RBV1/RBAW tasks instead of duplicated.
Agent outcome evaluation measures localization, evidence completeness, patch scope and miss taxonomy without treating external checks as truth verdicts.
RepoBrief remains read-only for code understanding; patch, shell, test, sandbox and merge authority remain external.

metadata.source_thread: Lenskit / RepoBrief optimization: agent-only code empowerment

lenskit_live_observation.status_note: Checkout r7 was ahead 1 / behind 1 against origin/main and had untracked .grabowski; plan registration therefore avoids Lenskit mutation.

metadata.boundary_decision: Agent empowerment means better evidence-guided code handling, not patch/test/shell authority inside RepoBrief core.
metadata.lifecycle_repair_note: Aligned initiative state with Bureau lifecycle diagnostics after task state changes.
